verse.verse_0: In the name of Allah, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful.
verse.verse_2: And those [clouds] carrying a load [of water]
verse.verse_5: Indeed, what you are promised is true.
verse.verse_6: And indeed, the recompense is to occur.
verse.verse_8: Indeed, you are in differing speech.
verse.verse_9: Deluded away from the Qur'an is he who is deluded.
verse.verse_11: Who are within a flood [of confusion] and heedless.
verse.verse_12: They ask, "When is the Day of Recompense?"
verse.verse_13: [It is] the Day they will be tormented over the Fire
verse.verse_14: [And will be told], "Taste your torment. This is that for which you were impatient."
verse.verse_15: Indeed, the righteous will be among gardens and springs,
verse.verse_16: Accepting what their Lord has given them. Indeed, they were before that doers of good.
verse.verse_17: They used to sleep but little of the night,
verse.verse_18: And in the hours before dawn they would ask forgiveness,
verse.verse_19: And from their properties was [given] the right of the [needy] petitioner and the deprived.
verse.verse_20: And on the earth are signs for the certain [in faith]
verse.verse_21: And in yourselves. Then will you not see?
verse.verse_22: And in the heaven is your provision and whatever you are promised.
verse.verse_23: Then by the Lord of the heaven and earth, indeed, it is truth - just as [sure as] it is that you are speaking.
verse.verse_24: Has there reached you the story of the honored guests of Abraham? -
verse.verse_25: When they entered upon him and said, "[We greet you with] peace." He answered, "[And upon you] peace, [you are] a people unknown.
verse.verse_26: Then he went to his family and came with a fat [roasted] calf
verse.verse_27: And placed it near them; he said, "Will you not eat?"
verse.verse_28: And he felt from them apprehension. They said, "Fear not," and gave him good tidings of a learned boy.
verse.verse_29: And his wife approached with a cry [of alarm] and struck her face and said, "[I am] a barren old woman!"
verse.verse_30: They said, "Thus has said your Lord; indeed, He is the Wise, the Knowing."
verse.verse_31: [Abraham] said, "Then what is your business [here], O messengers?"
verse.verse_32: They said, "Indeed, we have been sent to a people of criminals
verse.verse_33: To send down upon them stones of clay,
verse.verse_34: Marked in the presence of your Lord for the transgressors."
verse.verse_35: So We brought out whoever was in the cities of the believers.
verse.verse_36: And We found not within them other than a [single] house of Muslims.
verse.verse_37: And We left therein a sign for those who fear the painful punishment.
verse.verse_38: And in Moses [was a sign], when We sent him to Pharaoh with clear authority.
verse.verse_39: But he turned away with his supporters and said," A magician or a madman."
verse.verse_40: So We took him and his soldiers and cast them into the sea, and he was blameworthy.
verse.verse_41: And in 'Aad [was a sign], when We sent against them the barren wind.
verse.verse_42: It left nothing of what it came upon but that it made it like disintegrated ruins.
verse.verse_43: And in Thamud, when it was said to them, "Enjoy yourselves for a time."
verse.verse_44: But they were insolent toward the command of their Lord, so the thunderbolt seized them while they were looking on.
verse.verse_45: And they were unable to arise, nor could they defend themselves.
verse.verse_46: And [We destroyed] the people of Noah before; indeed, they were a people defiantly disobedient.
verse.verse_47: And the heaven We constructed with strength, and indeed, We are [its] expander.
verse.verse_48: And the earth We have spread out, and excellent is the preparer.
verse.verse_49: And of all things We created two mates; perhaps you will remember.
verse.verse_50: So flee to Allah. Indeed, I am to you from Him a clear warner.
verse.verse_51: And do not make [as equal] with Allah another deity. Indeed, I am to you from Him a clear warner.
verse.verse_52: Similarly, there came not to those before them any messenger except that they said, "A magician or a madman."
verse.verse_53: Did they suggest it to them? Rather, they [themselves] are a transgressing people.
verse.verse_54: So leave them, [O Muhammad], for you are not to be blamed.
verse.verse_55: And remind, for indeed, the reminder benefits the believers.
verse.verse_56: And I did not create the jinn and mankind except to worship Me.
verse.verse_57: I do not want from them any provision, nor do I want them to feed Me.
verse.verse_58: Indeed, it is Allah who is the [continual] Provider, the firm possessor of strength.
verse.verse_59: And indeed, for those who have wronged is a portion [of punishment] like the portion of their predecessors, so let them not impatiently urge Me.
verse.verse_60: And woe to those who have disbelieved from their Day which they are promised.
